title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Japp summons Hercule Poirot to apartment number 45 in the Robbott King building, overlooking Betsy Park, to investigate a discovery. Inside a small storeroom, they find the decomposed body of Mabel Sainsbury-Sil inside a large metal fur box. Although the body is unrecognizable due to facial destruction, Poirot identifies her by a green wool coat and a shabby shoe with a tarnished buckle.

Japp explains that the apartment belongs to Sylvia Chappman, a reclusive, well-dressed blonde woman. A doorman had previously seen Miss Sainsbury-Sil visiting Mrs. Chappman, but he only realized her identity after seeing a classified advertisement and remembering her green coat and shoe buckles. Inspector Baddesley discovered that Mrs. Chappman had disappeared over a month prior, leaving only a note for her maid, Nellie, stating she was going out. Baddesley also found a bloodstain on the carpet and a closed leather suitcase containing the victim's body, her handbag, and a letter. Japp suggests the face may have been destroyed to cover the victim's identity or as an act of revenge, though he notes the identity remained discoverable.

Poirot inspects Mrs. Chappman's bedroom, which is covered in dust and shows no signs of a hasty departure, suggesting the room was carefully cleaned. Upon examining the wardrobe, Poirot finds a collection of high-quality fur coats, including a gray squirrel coat from the box where the body was found, as well as trendy size-five sandals. In the bathroom, Poirot observes various cosmetics and hair dyes, leading him to conclude that Mrs. Chappman likely dyes her hair to maintain her blonde appearance.
